subject: Re: Issues trying to run BoxNet2
body:
Hi,

I am experiencing the exact same problem. I have run my tests with the 
EMPIAR-10164 data following the "Teamtomo walkthtough" tutorial on two 
different machines ([1] Win11, 128 Gb RAM, 24 cores, 1x Geforce RTX 4090 
and  [2] Win10, 128 Gb RAM, 24 cores, 2x Geforce GTX 1080). Apart from 
GoldNet picking zero particles, everything seems to run fine.

I have checked also the original 1.09 version of Warp as well as the latest 
nightly update.

I am unsure whether this might be a soft- or hardware related issue. From 
my reading I suspect that the 'Lovelace' Geforce RTX 4090 might be 
problematic with regard to Pytorch and downloaded models for the picker. 
Anyhow, the 'Pascal' GTX 1080 setup seems to have the same problem ...

Any help appreciated!

Kyle Messina schrieb am Donnerstag, 2. November 2023 um 22:43:00 UTC+1:

> Hello,
>
> Currently I'm trying to use Boxnet to mask gold fiducials but I seem to be 
> having issues getting Boxnet to work in general.  Essentially whenever I 
> run any pretrained Boxnet model (any of the ones in the public repository 
> or Goldnet), no particles are found in any micrograph.  There is no error 
> or crash after running the models, it loads the model pretty quickly, and 
> analyzes the micrographs to report that no particles are found.  
>
> Currently we are running Warp 1.0.9 and I had to download the Boxnet 
> models via the Public Repository so its possible I may have downloaded the 
> files to the wrong locations.  Any advice or help troubleshooting would be 
> greatly appreciated.  
>
